name: length_drops_to_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_length_drops_to_zero<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    new_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail == remove_index,
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
        new_self.spec_len() == old_self.spec_len() - 1,
    ensures
        new_self@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if removing one element from a list with ≥2 elements could empty it entirely

name: old_list_allows_singleton
code:
proof fn phi_2_old_list_allows_singleton<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail == remove_index,
    ensures
        old_self@.len() <= 1,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if the preconditions fail to guarantee the list has at least 2 elements

name: removed_value_still_present
code:
proof fn phi_3_removed_value_still_present<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    new_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail == remove_index,
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self.unique(),
        new_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
        new_self.spec_len() == old_self.spec_len() - 1,
    ensures
        new_self@.contains(ret),
{
}
reason: Undesirable if the removed value persists in the post-state sequence despite unique removal

name: sequence_unchanged_after_remove
code:
proof fn phi_4_sequence_unchanged_after_remove<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    new_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail == remove_index,
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
        new_self.spec_len() == old_self.spec_len() - 1,
    ensures
        new_self@ =~= old_self@,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if the abstract sequence is identical before and after removal, meaning nothing was actually removed

name: free_list_size_unchanged
code:
proof fn phi_5_free_list_size_unchanged<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    new_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len != 1,
        old_self.free_list_len != 0,
        old_self.value_list_tail == remove_index,
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
        ret == v,
        new_self.spec_len() == old_self.spec_len() - 1,
    ensures
        new_self.free_list_len == old_self.free_list_len,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if the free list doesn't grow by 1 when a node is removed from the value list, violating the N = free + value invariant
